etymology.description: semantic 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") + phonetic 己(己) (jǐ) -> record; chronicle
etymology.interpretation: Silk 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") symbolizes threads or lines, which are integral to weaving or recording information. The phonetic component 己(己) (jǐ) contributes the sound. Together, they evoke the concept of creating a record, akin to weaving threads into a cohesive document or chronicle.
etymology.simplification: 纪 is simplified by replacing 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") with its simpler form 纟, while retaining 己(己) (jǐ) as the phonetic component.

examples.chinese: 这本书记录了一个王朝的历史(這本書記錄了一個王朝的歷史)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū jìlùle yī gè wángcháo de lìshǐ.
examples.english: This book chronicles the history of a dynasty.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢写日记(他喜歡寫日記)，用来记录生活(用來記錄生活)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān xiě rìjì, yòng lái jìlù shēnghuó.
examples.english: He likes to write a diary to record his life.

examples.chinese: 这个博物馆收藏了许多古代文物(這個博物館收藏了許多古代文物)，记录了人类发展的历史(記錄了人類發展的歷史)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège bówùguǎn shōucángle xǔduō gǔdài wénwù, jìlùle rénlèi fāzhǎn de lìshǐ.
examples.english: This museum houses many ancient artifacts that chronicle the history of human development.
